errors: The ELM implementation has several discrepancies with the CPG requirements. 1. The CPG states the minimum age is 18, while the ELM implementation uses 25. 2. The CPG specifies a lookback period of 12 months, but the ELM implementation uses 6 months. 3. The CPG’s “No Screening Needed If” condition is implemented as “NOT (EXISTS (EncounterLookBack(Retrieve Encounter where type in ValueSet "Encounter to Screen for Blood Pressure VS", 6 months)))” which is equivalent to 6 months, not 12 months as specified in the CPG.
The ELM implementation has several mismatches with the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG requires Age >= 15 years AND Age <= 65 years, while the ELM implementation uses Age >= 30 years. This significantly alters the screening recommendation. The time interval is correct (1 year). The value set reference for "HIV Test" is also correct.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG states “Age >= 18 years”, while the ELM implementation uses “Age >= 25 years”. This violates the inclusion criteria specified in the CPG. Additionally, the “Has Recent Alcohol Screening” logic is overly restrictive. The CPG states “No Screening Needed If: An alcohol misuse screening (from “Alcohol Use Screening” value set - `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542`) has been performed within the past 1 year with a result present”. The ELM implementation only checks if *any* observation exists in the specified value set within the past year, regardless of the result. This doesn't accurately reflect the CPG's requirement that the screening *result* be present.
